summary: Dome FSF formula pinned by direct citation. Source: check_eclipse_fsf.py (dome repo) defines fsf = (h/r) / (h_b/r_bou) — purely geometric. Inputs are station dome-coordinate radius r and dome H(r) profile; eclipse magnitude is NOT an input (coverage enters the outer equation ΔB = baseline × coverage × FSF as a separate multiplicative factor). Published E001–E009 FSF values (EBR 1.029, HAD 1.471, LER 2.075) reproduce to ≤3% under V51 ellipse H(r) with V51 station radii, but NOT under V50.6 exponential H(r) with V50.6 radii (HAD computes 1.89 vs published 1.47). This pins Section 1.9 P2 load-bearing sentence (‘scaling formula consumes shadow geometry, not magnitudes’) and strengthens WIN-044 Self-Contradicted verdict with a code-level receipt. Recommended: (1) patch Section 1.9 P2 to replace assertion with direct FSF code quote, (2) append FSF formula to WIN-044 detail_evidence, (3) record dome commit SHA at citation time. Patch texts included in EXP-165.json under section_1_9_patch and win044_patch.
